Neighborhood Overview: De Baarsjes is a young and creative neighborhood which has become more and more popular over the past few years. I love it for the great mix of people -every age group and background is represented-, and the local public amenities reflect that variety. You will find cheap local grocery stores and a brand new mosque alongside beautiful design stores and great coffee places.
Transit: To reach my house from Central Station, you catch tram 13 (direction Geuzenveld) to tramstop Admiraal de Ruyterweg, which takes approximately 12 minutes. From there, it's a 5 minute walk to my house.  To reach my house from Schiphol, you catch a train to Amsterdam Lelylaan (7 mins) and take tram 17 (direction Centraal Station) to tramstop 'Witte de Withstraat' (6 mins). From the tramstop, it's a 5 min walk to my house. If you're travelling by car, there's usually plenty of space to park in front of or nearby the house. There's paid parking (2,40/hour) from Monday til Saturday 09h-24h. You can reach the city center (f.e. Dam Square) by foot in about 30 minutes. If you'd rather explore the city the way the locals do -by bike!- there's several bike rental shops closeby.
Access: As a guest, you have access to the living room, kitchen, bathroom, toilet, laundry room and balcony. Mi casa es su casa.
Interaction: If you need any help during your stay, whether organising your public transport to the airport or finding a great local place to have dinner, I'm happy to help.
House Rules: No pets allowed, no smoking in the house, but feel free to smoke on the balcony. As I live in a quiet street, this is not a place for partying in the house.

Neighborhood Overview: Ano-Petralona is the new up-coming hip area of Athens. Located at the South part of Acropolis, under the Sacred Rock, next to Thesio sidewalk and across the Gazi area the heart of Athens nightlife . It's a friendly, safe and calm area with an ambience of the old traditional Athens with a combination of beautiful old houses as well as modern buildings. In the high part of the district, just one street above the street where the apartment is located (Troon Str.), you will find many traditional taverns as well as international cuisine restaurants and bars. In addition, if you are film-lovers you can visit the famous Zephyrous open-air cinema. Don't miss to stroll around the narrow streets of the neighborhood and have a nice walk at the peripheral of Filoppapou leading to the Acropolis museum.
Transit: The apartment is accessible by train, metro and taxis. In case you want to rent a car, there is a lot of free parking space.
Access: Train station of Ano-Petralona is only two minutes away. It's the green line No1 that connects Piraeus with Kifissia and it's just one station from famous Thesio & Monastiraki (flee market). Also, metro station of Keramikos is only 15 minutes walk. Taxis are available at any time.

   When going abroad, I consider myself a traveler, not a tourist. The difference between those two is that a traveler doesn’t need to go with the mass but to explore himself. In order to do so, he needs to know that before the exploration and afterwards, there will always be a spotless house to rest, with warm bed, soft fresh linens and a comfortable kitchen. What a traveler needs is a home away from home.
By offering my places through airbnb I put my own delight in a first place. In each traveler I see myself when being abroad and think what was that missing from my own trips. For this reason, I decorated all of my places on my own and I was thrilled about the future guests. This satisfaction makes the hosting experience a hobby, and never a job.
   It is a great honor for me to receive your enjoyment when staying at my apartment. You are always welcome!

Neighborhood Overview: Ermou Street took its name after Hermes, the ancient Greek God later equated with the Romans’ Mercury – the protector of traders. Indeed, this is Athens’ most popular commercial street. Here, you will find everything that a satisfying shopping spree might require, from a huge selection of footwear and clothing to eye glasses, housewares and numerous other useful items. Being the primary path on foot between Syntagma and Monastiraki squares, there are always people on Ermou and during shopping hours the street can be full of shoppers. It is also full of street musicians, mimes, clowns and street merchants. There are coffee shops on many of the side streets as well as fast food places where you can get burgers, souvlaki, tiropita, spanakopita and sandwiches or you can get roast corn or chestnuts. With fashion shops and medium sized malls promoting most international brands, it is in the top five most expensive shopping streets in Europe, and the tenth most expensive retail street in the
Transit: If you start at the top of Syntagma Square and walk down the steps and past the water fountain, at the bottom of the square is the beginning of Ermou Street, a paradise for those who live to shop. From the Parliament building you can make a straight line that follows Ermou all the way to Gazi, the old gas works of Athens, now the center of its nightlife. Ermou street is a commercial avenue which has been turned into a pedestrian only street. It is the primary clothing shopping street for Athens and contains many of the big international brands. The Parthenon, the Temple of Zeus, the Museum of Acropolis and Plaka are only 10 minutes walk from the apartment.  All public transportation and taxis are literally at your doorstep.

Neighborhood Overview: Apartment's location makes it unbeatable. It's located exactly in the city center, just 10 minutes from the most famous pedestrian road of Athens (Ermou) which starts from Monastiraki metro station and ends at the Syntagma square in front of the parliament! Plaka area is in front of your door. Acropolis, Parthenon, and the Ancient Agora where the heart of old Athens beats is under your feet! This area is for sure the best place to stay when visiting the city for many reasons. Also, you will be staying 15 min away on foot from a very nice area "Kolonaki", which is said to be the posh area of Athens with many bars and local food stores as well as 20 minutes from "Exarcheia" which is Athens' alternative scene.
Notes: What I like the most of this area is the fact that it is always alive, full of things to do, on a walking distance. Whatever you might need is on your feet. All the best brands of the big chain shops are down below your window. Acropolis is a 10-15 mins walk. Plaka, the small village under the Acropolis, is practically your neighborhood, right across the corner. Movies, theaters, Acropolis museum, the best restaurants of the city, all are around. Lately this area became the place to be, for its nightlife as well. The hippiest bars and tavernas of the city are located here. This is the place, us Greeks, we go out the most, during the nights as well. Another plus of staying here is security. Being so close to the Parliament and the central square of the city means that Police is always around. The truth is, that there are no new apartments in this area as it is the preserved old historical center. Nearby there are the great big luxury hotels of Athens, like Grande Bretagne Hotel and King
Transit: Transportation is a main reason. The area is very well serviced by many metro stations, bus and tram. There are at least 3 metro stations near the apartment including the central metro station of the city, where the airport train arrives and the bus from the airport as well. There is also the one and only tram line of the city departing close by, which it can transports you to the south suburbs of Athens, where the best beaches are. Transport to/from Airport and Piraeus Port provided upon request.
Access: Situated in the liveliest area of Athens, most favorite among tourists, spot of the city ''Thisio square'', just 300 meters from the "Monastiraki square" and the famous ''Apostolou Pavlou'' pedestrian str. which leads to the Acropolis! The Metro station ''Kerameikos'' (line 3) and ''Thisio'' (line 1), is just 200meters that connects you directly with Athens International Airport and the port of Piraeus. All nightlife bars and restaurants are nearby. All monuments are obviously under your feet. Acropolis museum is a pleasant 10 - 15 mins walk through the famous ''Apostolou Pavlou pedestrian str.'' Choosing my apartment for your exquisite and quality stay, you can visit on foot the most important historical sites of Athens. Furthermore, you are only 15 minutes walking distance from Kolonaki, the most chic shopping area of Greece. Equally, the hip and trendy Gkazi area with its boasting nightlife is only 5 mins walk. Neighborhood Overview: Makrianni was really put on the map when the Acropolis Museum opened, along with the Acropolis metro station. Before that it was like any other area of Athens with its apartment buildings and lack of green space. But the park-ification plans of Athens were good to the neighborhood and it is now one of the most popular walking areas in the city. It is hard to remember the days when both Makrianni Street and D Areopagitou were choked with buses, cars and carbon-monoxide and the Plaka stopped abruptly at the end of Byronas street. But now it is a continuation of walk-able Athens, not to mention ride-able Athens on Segeways and skateboards, bicycles and other more traditional means of transportation. The neighborhood has three major sites, the first being of course the Acropolis which rises out of the trees and ruins of the ancient temples below it. The second is the new Acropolis Museum, waiting patiently for the return of the Elgin Marbles and attracting thousands of visitors a day. The
Notes: About the Owner: The Owner of the penthouse is a fashion designer who is passionate about ancient Greek history. She was the interior designer of the penthouse. The design combines contemporary granite, parquet floors and furniture with art nouveau stained glass cupboards, inlaid art nouveau tile and vintage ceiling lights. The result is a warm, atmospheric space which is equally luxurious and cosy. The owner acquired the property when she fell in love with Acropolis view which she describes as a 'priceless painting'.

Neighborhood Overview: The studio is located on one of the top neighborhoods to live in the city. It's an elegant upscale downtown quarter, where old Athens meets modern Athens.  It's unique characteristic is tranquility in the heart of the city. At a walking distance one can find the busy Ermou, the High Street with retail shops, restaurants bars and coffee shops, but also the Hill of the Nymphs where one can enjoy a serene walk and enjoy nature and the panoramic views of Athens.   Numerous archaeological sites and museums are close by and even a refreshing swim at the beach can be easily reached by Public Transport.  The area is proposed as the ideal solution to those who want to explore all surrounding places of historic interest such as Plaka, Monastiraki, Thissio, the Ancient Agora of Athens. Furthermore, within 2 minutes walk from the house one can find restaurants, supermarkets, pharmacies, some of the best bakeries in Athens, e.t.c.

Through a network of pedestrian streets ( Dionysiou Areopagitou Apostolou Pavlou, Ermou, Adrianou, etc) one can visit Thission, the Ancient Agora, the Keramikos Cemetery while the National Archaelogical Museum is easily reached by Public Transport.  From Syggrou-Fix Station one can easily take the tram to visit the Athenian RIiviera, the South Coast of Athens with many recreational facilities, (beaches, restaurants, bars, yacht clubs, marinas). Neighborhood Overview: Walking distance to the center of Athens and to the many sights (National Gallery, Panathinaiko marble Stadium, Zappion, Temple of Olympic Zeus, Acropolis, War Museum, Byzantine Museum, Museum of Cycladic Art, National Gardens, etc.). You also have many transportation choices: metro, buses, etc. if you are not into walking. Very close to the National Gardens and other Parks, ideal for running or walking. The area is safe for walking and central.
Notes: The place is quiet, so you will enjoy your sleep and truly relax. I have also made available for you honey, jam, pasta, beer, wine and other food & drinks to make you feel at home and enjoy your stay. I believe that they are sufficient for your first few days in Athens.
Transit: Public transportation is available on the spot. Buses pass from the main roads around the apartment and take you to the center and other areas of Athens. The metro/underground is a 10' walk. The Athens Hilton is a 10' walk, as well. I would suggest that you walk to the center of the city in a 20' walk around some of the sights and museum. In the morning I would suggest to take a walk to the center, through the National Gardens. It is one of the nicest walks of the city. Typical walking distance times for the following sights: National Gallery 10' National Gardens 15' Panathinaiko Stadium 15' War Museum 13' Benjamin Museum 21' Cycladic Art Museum 2 Zappio 22' Temple of Olympic Zeus 25' Acropolis Museum 30' Taxis are also easy to find and they would cost you only a few euros to take you to all the main sights.
Access: You have full access to the whole apartment which includes the living room, kitchen, one bedroom and one bathroom.
Interaction: You can interact with me anytime you have a question or you need advice/suggestions on what to see, where to go, where to eat and how to enjoy your stay in Athens.
House Rules: - You are free to eat/use the supplies (food, wine, beer, etc.), I have made available for you. I will be happy to recommend you where to buy similar ones, to take home with you. - Please respect the "quiet hours" of the day 15:00-17:30 and 22:00-07:30. - Please do not remove items from the place. They are available for all the guests to use them.

It is safe, it is local, it gives you the glimpse of true Greek life. Same time being very conveniently near everything a tourist comes to see in Athens :)  and those concerned about safety- you could not choose better area, its ok even to walk at midnight- no worries:) Architecture itself is modest- typical Greek residential area, but really very safe (even at night!) and easy connected with centre. Syntagma is 10 min by trolleybus, 20 min by walk, or 5 min by taxi (5 euros- if you are asked more, then you have an dishonest driver) same to Acropolis Very easy route to and from AIRPORT (bus X95 to Syntagma and then 5 eur Taxi) and ship port- instructions provided in itinerary. AIRPORT bus X95- ticket 6 eur to Syntagma and then short taxi ride (5 min and 6 eur) Bus X95 from airport runs 24/7 and bus 40 to ship port also. If you wish a private driver- ask from us- we may be able to help with this.

There is also Mac computer for our guests to use. The other residents in this apartment are also clean and polite tourists.  You will be welcome to use all common areas and facilities, but are also kindly asked to be respectful with other guests, be neat.  The whole apartment is strictly non smoking. We expect our guests to clean and put things back after each use.

Mike & Vaki are your hosts.

Vaki is a professional like Mike. She works with Mike in the translation and localization industry and at the same time she is involved in Airbnb business. She is Mikes hands and legs- she will be helping you here in Athens. She loves literature, traveling and animal welfare.

Name: Spacious Loft (73m²) with Large Roof Terrace
Summary: Welcome to my sunny loft apartment (73m²) above the roofs of Neukölln. The layout of the apartment is unique offering views to both street as well as the inner court yard side. A big window front opens onto a large green roof terrace. Underground train is only five minutes walking. Bars, restaurants shops are within walking distance. This is my own home where I live myself. Not one of these soulless Ikea furnished holiday rentals. Please, treat it the same way as you would treat your own.
Space: The apartment is on 5th floor just under the roof. There's an elevator if you don't feel like walking. What I like a lot about this apartment is that in summer it doesn't get hot. Because you can open windows on both sides of the living room, you can always create a draft that cools down the apartment in an instant. Trust me! One of the highlights is definitely the large roof terrace. This year I got lots of plants for it and it's perfect for a breakfast in the sun. Feel free to use anything in the kitchen for cooking etc. The washing machine is also quite intuitive - but I don't have a dryer, so you need to hang your clothes.

Mit öffentlichen Verkehrsmitteln ist alles super gut innerhalb 20 Minuten zu erreichen. Auch das Tempelhoferfeld ist ganz nah! Wir sind eine 3-er Wg im alter von 21-25. Die Wohnung ist einetypische Berliner Altbauwohnung mit Dielen und Stuck. Die Wohnung besteht aus einem großen Wohnzimmer was ihr nutzen könnt sowie drei Schlafzimmer, eine große Küche und ein Bad. Wir haben einen Balkon der aber zur Straße raus geht. ------------------------ If you want to get to know Berlin in a non-touristic way, you will love Neukölln. The flat consists out of a 3 bedrooms, living room, kitchen and bathroom. You are able to use one bedroom, the livingroom and the big kitchen.

The sofa can be changed into a bed for one or two children, the place is pretty family friendly too.

The party area is far enough away but in walking distance. Bus and S-Bahn are near, and theres a supermarket next door. You'll have a kitchen, a bath with bathtub, and a native Berlin-expert to answer all your questions. The sofa can be changed into a bed for one or two children, the place is pretty family friendly too. If you're coming from Schönefeld we recommend taking the bus 171 (direction Hermannplatz) directly from the airport to Werrastraße, wich is just around the corner. This will take you about 50 minutes, so a bit longer,  but its a lot less stress and has better views. The place is close to the corner of Weser- and Treptowerstraße. The next stations are S-Bahn Sonnenallee and the buses 171 and M41.

Neighborhood Overview: The neighborhood is upmarket and luxurous but still very Dutch. We are centrally located just around the corner of a popular small high-end shopping street with little trendy boutique shops and many small restaurants for breakfast, lunch and dinner, deli, bakery, grocery. It's easy to grab a coffee around the corner. You can visit the small local farmers market on saturday. Kids can easily play in the different playgrounds of the Vondelpark as our house is just 2 streets - 5 min. away from the park. It is a great experience to stay in a very Dutch neighborhood and still be close to the major museums such as the Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh museum, Concert hall and the canals. Typical for this neighbourhood are also a the terraces where people from the neighboorhood love to sit outside enjoying a coffee or a glass of wine or a good meal. Transit: Our house is located near a tram stop which brings you to the Museum Quarter in 2 minutes or 7 mins to the canal area and 15 mins to Central Station. 15 minutes walk (through the Vondelpark) to Leidseplein (which is the going out area in the center a lot of tourists visit); 25-30 min walk from the Dam (central square close to red light district); 10-15 min walk to Van Gogh museum, Rijksmuseum, House of Bols (cocktail museum); 20 min walk to "de Pijp” area where the locals go for beers;  the Heineken museum and the famous daily Albert Cuyp market. A taxi directly from Schiphol to the Valeriusstraat will cost you around 40 euro’s. Bikes can be rented in the small bike shop in the Emmastraat.

Hello,
We love AIrbnb. It is a great concept. We are proud te be a member of this community and love to explore the world at other Airbnb nice places. We introduce ourselves.
We arrived in Amsterdam in 1992 ( Michiel) and 1994 (Marlous) for doing our study and since then we didn’t leave this amazing city (except all the travels and work experiences abroad) so we discovered the city at early age ( with experiencing all the good and bad things), started our career over here and now we have our family life in this lovely neighborhood which feels like a modern upgraded ghetto in the middle of the energetic city of Amsterdam.

We enjoy our family life with the kids who goes to school and has there friends nearby and playgrounds are all over around (our garden, “Vondelpark”, “Museumplein”) and within a second we leave the house for a movie (at Cinecenter Cinema near "Leidseplein" is our favorite) or a theatre (Stadsschouwburg at "Leidseplein" which also has lots of International Plays) or having drinks with friends in one of the bars or restaurant nearby (the “Pijp” or the Jordaan”). All within a short distance or a 10 or 20 minute walk. 

We love to work hard as an Artist Manager and an Advocate and doing sports like racecycling, jogging, yoga and we all play tennis. We do love art and painting. We loved to travel around the world and now with our kids we downsized the distance to overcome and explore Europe with his nice countries like Italia (our favorite till so far is the lovely place Lucca) and Austria (for skieing and staying in a nice family village like Zell am See), Turkey (with the charming surfing hotspot of Alacati) and our favorite cities like Paris ( we love to eat a croissant with our kids in Parque Luxembourg) and Tarifa (for hanging around in this kite-surfing place paradise). We also enjoy simply going to the nearby beaches of Bloemendaal (Beachclub Republiek), Zandvoort ( Family Beach Vooges) or Noordwijk (Beachclub Zuiderbad) and we love to overstay a weekend in our familyhouse in Zeeland. We enjoying life with our friends and being with our lovely kids.

We are also interested in Houseswap with another family for a nice place which is child friendly, has 5 beds available, situated nearby sea and beach or nice cities in Europa, a lovely apartment in New York or anything special and authentique. Don't hesistate to contact us!

Neighborhood Overview: Built during the city’s Golden Age, the three parallel canals are interconnected with streets filled with shopping, eating and drinking opportunities. The Canal Ring is made up of Herengracht, Keizersgracht and Prinsengracht. In a way, the Canal Ring forms a crossover from the more bustling Centre to the gentler outer neighbourhoods of Jordaan, Museum District and De Pijp. The Canal Ring includes the city’s two main nightlife and culture squares. Leidseplein features such legendary concert halls as Paradiso and Melkweg. Rembrandtplein has several bars, two large nightclubs (Escape and Air), and ‘old world’ evocative treasures such as De Kroon and Café Schiller. The whole area is dotted with Amsterdam's beloved neighbourhood ‘brown cafes'. Distance to:  Central Station: 1.5km  Dam: 0.8km  Leidseplein: 1km  Museumplein: 1.8km  Vondelpark: 1.4km

If the apartment is available we offer a late check at 14:00 for an additional charge of 20 euro.

Tram 4 and 9 also both go from Utrechtsestraat and can take you  to Central Station and Dam Square or to for example the Amsterdam RAI or Albert Cuyp Market. The easiest and best way to get around Amsterdam is by walking around or to rent a bike. You will the vibe while you travel around the city.

Neighborhood Overview: A formerly working-class neighbourhood, today ike the neighboring district Jordaan , de Pijp is brimming with fantastic cafés, restaurants, coffee shops and bars. De Pijp is home to a multicultural community and the city's largest market. The colourful sights and scents of the Albert Cuypmarkt are sure to delight and the area teems with charming ethnic restaurants, cosy brown cafés and trendy bars and terraces. De Pijp is most famous for the Albert Cuypmarkt.This daily market has been offering its wares for more than a century and vendors sell everything from fresh seafood, meat and cheese to flowers, clothes and jewellery. Stroll past the colourful stalls and sample the street food for a real Pijp experience. With its multitude of ethnic restaurants and trendy bars, de Pijp will leave you spoiled for choice. A good combination of great food and drink can be found at Bazar, with its Middle Eastern and North African menu served in a church that's been converted into an Oriental fairy ta

Neighborhood Overview: You will find this beautiful, just renovated, apartment in the best neighbourhood of Amsterdam: The (De) Pijp. The Pijp is know for its trendy bars, cafés and restaurants, Heineken Brewery and the famous Albert Cuyp market. The museum district (Van Gogh museum, Stedelijk museum and Rijksmuseum) is only 5 minutes away. The nieuwe Pijp as this area is called has an idyllic character with houses designed by the Amsterdam School style.  Close to your home stands the famous Okura Hotel. The roof of the Okura Hotel is the biggest barometer of Netherlands 75 m height. The color of the light that shines on the canopy, is the indication for the weather forecast for the next day. Blue means sunny weather ahead, green adverse weather and white changeable weather.  In front of the Okura hotel you can rent a boat to tour the canals.

Today it is home to a multicultural community of expats, students, hipsters & immigrants and to one of the city's most famous markets. The colourful sights and many fragrances of the Albert Cuypmarkt are sure to delight and the area teems with a variety of charming restaurants, cosy brown cafés and trendy bars that will challenge your senses and flood your taste buds. Also, the famous Heineken Museum draws many visitors to de Pijp every day.

Neighborhood Overview: The famous ‘’De Pijp’’ is one of the most cosmopolitan and vibrant neighborhoods in the South of Amsterdam, but it is close to the City Centre. Formerly it was a working class quarter. It is a very diverse neighborhood with more then 100 different nationalities. The van Woustraat is one of the commercial streets of this district, with many different restaurants, bars and shops. These streets are always busy, but through the whole district you will find coffee shops and a lot of different ethnic food. You cannot visit the de Pijp without visiting the Albert Cuyp market, here you will find more than 100 shops. It is a real Dutch market with many stalls with vegetables, cheese, fish and clothes. The Sarphatipark is located in this district, it is a beautiful urban park. You can escape the busy city and go for a nice walk. The famous Heineken Experience is also located in this district. I think it's not a once, but a 'more in a lifetime' opportunity to rent your house and meet other people from around the world. Not as a tourist in a hotel or bar, but in your own apartment, letting other people have the same feeling as I have: living in Amsterdam makes me feel good, every day again. 
Who am I? I'm 29 years old and work as a radiologist in the hospital. It's a very interesting and developing job which has a lot challenges. It's hard work but very satisfying. Besides work, I embrace the social life and most of my free time you can find me with friends, doing what Amsterdam has to offer (it's quite a lot), from visiting the newest bars and clubs in town 'till art/photo fairs or musea. In summer you'll find me at music festivals or drink some beers in and around the canals. Yeah, in the canal but in a boot! 
I wintertime, Amsterdam gets (again) very cosy and 'gezellig' (use the term when you're here). All the small bars, cafe's and restaurants are helping me surviving the winter. A couple of times during the winter I escape Amsterdam, and visit the Alps for some skiing. 
Since I'm living in Amsterdam for quite some years now, I know every little corner in the city, so if you need some advice on where to go, just ask me and I'll give you a dozen options.

Enjoy your stay!

Neighborhood Overview: De Pijp is vibrant and very much on its way up. As can be expected of an area that is centred on the nation’s most famous outdoor market: Albert Cuypmarkt, near to the Heineken brewery. This Amsterdam neighbourhood is famously diverse with over 100 nationalities represented. While still the best neighbourhood to choose from a wide selection of ethnic food, more high-end bars and restaurants have come on the scene to service students and young professionals. Further east along De Pijp there is the Van Woustraat, a former fast food strip, there are now several of the city’s more highly regarded restaurants. But perhaps most representative of the area is Bazar, a former church on the Albert Cuypmarkt that has been reinvented as a funky North African café/restaurant. Distance to: Central station: 2 km Dam square: 1,5 km Museum square: 0,8 km

Summary: My lovely apartment, located next to the Westerpark, is perfect suitable for a couple or two friends that are looking for a nice and quiet area to stay, but still close to the heart of Amsterdam. The tram in front of my apartment takes you within 15 minutes to the city centre where you can shop along the famous canals or enjoy a culinairy experience in one of the many restaurants that our city offers.
Space: My cosy apartment is located next to the Westerpark and only a 15 minute tram ride away from the city centre. The apartment is on the 2nd floor of a shared building. There is no elevator. My home is decorated with light colours and Scandinavian design that will make you feel at home right away. Relax on the couch in the living room and enjoy the Netflix (and Dutch TV) on a large HD screen.  Have a cup of tea or an Italian ristretto and a Dutch stroopwafel in the kitchen. Cooking is not allowed, due to safety reasons, but you are welcome to use coffee and tea facilities.  The bedroom is located at the back of the apartment and has a comfortable bed (200 x 140 cm) with fresh sheets and two fluffy pillows. There is a chair, a table and large mirrors.  My bathroom has everything you need for a comfortable stay: There is a (rain)shower, a clean toilet and a sink with a mirror. 1 set of clean towels is provided per person per stay. Lastly, you'll have your own private balcony.

Neighborhood Overview: My apartment is located in the Staatsliederenbuurt (Amsterdam West). It's a nice and quiet neighbourhood close the centre. There is supermarkt (Albert Heijn) located  close to my apartment. Next to the supermarket you can find a drug store (Kruidvat) and a bakery. Visit the Westerpark for food and fun; You can have a great breakfast/lunch at the Bakkerswinkel, a drink at the Westergasterras or Pacific and a delicious dinner Mossel&Gin (Specialty: mussles & GT's). There are often events, parties or food markets at the Westerpark so have a look on their website for the upcoming events.
Notes: A few tips to help you out: - Buy tickets for the Anne Frank House upfront online, it saves you a lot of time - Leidse Square and Rembrandt Square are the worst places to get food - Visit the Rijksmuseum, it's amazing! - Even though  Amsterdam has one of the best pickpocket police squads in Europe, it's recommendable to watch your valuable items in the city centre and when getting off and on public transport
Transit: How to get to my apartment from Amsterdam Schiphol Airport: - Public transport €7 - €10: Take the train from Schiphol to Amsterdam Central Station (+/- 15 min.). From there take bus 21 (Geuzenveld) and get out at stop 'Van der Hoopstraat' (+/- 10 min.). From the bus stop my apartment is only 50 meters. - Taxi €30 - €40: Take an Uber or a taxi from the airport (+/- 20 min.) - Car: You can park in the surrounding area or €3 p/h. On Sunday parking is free. How to get around: Bus 21 (Central Station) takes you in 10 minutes to Central Station. From Central Station you can take the ferry to visit Amsterdam North or easily walk to Dam Square, Red Light District, China Town and the shopping area. Tram 10 (Azartplein) takes you in 10 min. to Leidse Square. Leidse Square offers a lot of restaurants, cafe's and shops. From here you can walk to all the famous Dutch museums, Rijksmuseum, van Gogh Museum etc.
